title: Plant Director - Debrecen EV Assembly
department: BMW Group Plant Debrecen - Vehicle Assembly
reports_to: Head of Production - EV Division, EMEA
role_summary:
BMW Group is seeking a Plant Director - Debrecen EV Assembly to lead one of the most strategically significant manufacturing operations in the company's transition to next-generation electric mobility. The role carries full leadership accountability for vehicle assembly performance at Plant Debrecen, including safety, quality, cost, delivery, launch stability, workforce engagement, and continuous improvement within the BMW Production System (BPS). The successful candidate will lead the plant through a critical phase of SOP preparation, ramp-up execution, and post-launch stabilization for Neue Klasse-related production.

This role requires a leader who can combine plant-level execution discipline with the judgment required to manage a high-visibility greenfield-like environment under real industrial pressure. The Plant Director will work closely with Quality, Logistics, Industrial Engineering, HR Manufacturing, Finance, and central production functions to ensure that assembly operations meet BMW Group expectations for premium manufacturing quality and operational robustness. Particular emphasis will be placed on quality gate discipline, launch governance, workforce capability building, and disciplined escalation of production risk.

The role is also central to the development of the local leadership culture, the integration of digital manufacturing tools, and the establishment of stable operating routines in a fast-scaling environment. Given the strategic importance of the site, the role demands strong communication with senior BMW stakeholders and effective handling of employee representation and labor-related matters. The successful candidate must be capable of delivering rapid performance improvement without sacrificing quality, workforce trust, or long-term plant maturity.
key_responsibilities: Lead all vehicle assembly operations at Plant Debrecen in line with BMW Group safety, quality, cost, delivery, and productivity targets.
Ensure disciplined launch readiness, SOP execution, and ramp-up stabilization for Neue Klasse-related assembly programs.
Implement and sustain BPS standards, daily performance routines, and structured escalation across plant operations.
Own quality gate execution and containment discipline in close partnership with Quality and Manufacturing Engineering.
Drive throughput, OEE, FTT, and takt adherence improvements while maintaining premium manufacturing standards.
Lead plant-level response to operational disruption, supplier-related instability, and launch-related bottlenecks.
Develop senior plant managers and frontline leadership capability to support sustainable performance.
Partner with Logistics and Supply Chain to manage material flow, line supply, and production continuity.
Lead workforce planning, capability development, and organization readiness in collaboration with HR Manufacturing.
Manage plant budget performance, resource allocation, and local investment priorities within approved frameworks.
Represent the assembly operation in regional and central BMW governance forums.
Ensure effective local engagement with employee representation bodies and labor stakeholders.
required_qualifications: University degree in engineering, manufacturing, industrial engineering, or a related technical discipline.
Substantial leadership experience in automotive assembly, vehicle manufacturing, or similarly complex industrial operations.
Proven track record in plant leadership, launch management, or major ramp-up execution.
Strong understanding of assembly operations, quality management, throughput control, and structured escalation.
Experience applying lean manufacturing or production-system methodologies in high-volume environments.
Demonstrated capability in people leadership at scale, including development of senior and frontline managers.
Strong stakeholder management across Quality, Supply Chain, HR, Finance, and Engineering.
Experience managing operational disruption, launch instability, or recovery situations in manufacturing environments.
Solid financial acumen including budget management and operational cost control.
Fluent English; German preferred; additional regional language capability is an advantage.
preferred_qualifications: Experience in EV assembly, battery-electric vehicle launch environments, or premium automotive manufacturing.
Exposure to greenfield or newly scaled manufacturing operations.
Experience with digital manufacturing tools, MES, or data-enabled shopfloor governance.
Track record in cross-cultural leadership within international manufacturing environments.
Experience in co-determined or labor-sensitive manufacturing settings.
Advanced executive education or senior manufacturing leadership program.
leadership_competencies: Builds a high-performance plant culture grounded in accountability, stability, and continuous improvement.
Leads decisively under launch pressure while maintaining quality discipline and workforce credibility.
Creates execution clarity through visible management routines, structured escalation, and strong team alignment.
Balances short-term plant recovery needs with long-term operational maturity and capability building.
Works effectively across functions and levels to remove bottlenecks and protect production performance.
Represents BMW Group's premium manufacturing standards in every aspect of plant leadership.

scope_and_scale.direct_reports: 8-12 senior plant and operational leadership reports
scope_and_scale.total_workforce: Approx. 3,500-5,000 employees across assembly and support functions
scope_and_scale.plants_overseen: Primary accountability for Debrecen assembly operations with strong interfaces to regional and central functions
scope_and_scale.key_programs: Neue Klasse SOP, launch stabilization, assembly performance maturity, digital shopfloor routines, workforce capability development

context_notes: The role is open due to the need for strengthened plant-level leadership ahead of a critical SOP and ramp-up phase at Debrecen. Immediate priorities include launch readiness, stable execution of assembly operations, quality containment discipline, development of local leadership capability, and establishment of resilient daily management routines suitable for a strategically important EV production site.
